title: Rocket Lab Just Became the Most Important Space Stock You Don't Own
subtitle: 87 launches, 250+ satellites deployed, and Neutron on the horizon. At $20B market cap, RKLB is the purest space-company asymmetrical bet in the market.
summary: Rocket Lab just hit 87 Electron launches, deployed 250+ satellites, and is building Neutron — a reusable medium-lift rocket. With end-to-end spacecraft manufacturing, growing government contracts, and a fraction of SpaceX's valuation, RKLB is the space stock that actually makes sense.

image.caption: Rocket Lab Electron launch — the second most frequently launched US rocket

title: Marvell Is the AI Semiconductor Giant Wall Street Is Ignoring
subtitle: Custom silicon. Optical interconnects. Data infrastructure. MRVL just ran from $53 to $189 — and it's still cheaper than Nvidia on every metric that matters.
summary: Marvell hit $189 after a 263% run from its 52-week low, posting $8.2B revenue and a 32.6% profit margin. With custom AI silicon exploding and optical interconnect demand surging, MRVL is the most underfollowed pure AI infrastructure play in semiconductors.

image.caption: Marvell Technology — powering AI data center infrastructure with custom silicon and optical interconnects

title: CoreWeave Is the AI Cloud Dark Horse Nobody's Talking About

summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) runs $6.2B in annual revenue as the premier GPU-as-a-service cloud for AI workloads. Up 50% YTD with a $138 analyst target, this is the infrastructure bet that captures AI compute demand without guessing which model wins.

title: Andrej Karpathy Just Joined Anthropic. Here's Why That Terrifies OpenAI
subtitle: The OpenAI co-founder and former Tesla AI leader is crossing enemy lines. Karpathy's move to Anthropic isn't just a talent grab — it's a declaration of war in the battle for AI supremacy.
summary: Andrej Karpathy — OpenAI co-founder, former Tesla AI director, and arguably the most recognizable AI researcher on the planet — just joined Anthropic to lead pretraining research. This isn't just a headline. It's the kind of talent shift that reshapes the competitive landscape of frontier AI. Anthropic gets a legend. OpenAI loses a founding father. The market should pay attention.

title: RTX Just Dipped 18% From Its Highs. Here's Why That's Your Entry Point.

summary: RTX dropped 18% from its $214 high despite posting $90B revenue, $7.2B FCF, and a growing dividend. At 25x forward earnings with a 28% analyst upside and defense budgets only expanding, this is the kind of dip you buy, not fear.

title: Meta Is Spending $65B on AI in 2026 — Smart Bet or Billionaire's Gamble?

summary: Meta's $65B AI capex plan has investors divided. With Llama 4 open-source AI gaining traction, Advantage+ ad revenue accelerating, and the metaverse pivot fading into the background, the bull case is stronger than ever — but the spending has no modern precedent.

title: Broadcom's AI Networking Dominance: Tomahawk, Custom ASICs, and the VMware Pivot
subtitle: While the market fixates on Nvidia GPUs, Broadcom quietly powers the networking fabric connecting every AI cluster. With Tomahawk 5, custom ASIC partnerships, and a VMware transformation unlocking recurring revenue, AVGO is becoming a compound machine.
summary: Broadcom's Tomahawk 5 switches connect Nvidia GPUs in every major hyperscaler cluster. Custom ASIC deals with Google, Meta, and Apple add $10B+ in AI revenue. VMware is pivoting to subscription, and the dividend just grew 18% — making AVGO a must-own in AI infrastructure.

title: AMD vs Nvidia in AI: Is 2026 Finally AMD's Year to Compete?
subtitle: With MI350 shipping, ROCm maturing, and hyperscalers demanding a second source, AMD is in its strongest competitive position ever. But Nvidia's CUDA moat and Blackwell ramp are formidable.
summary: AMD's Instinct MI350 is winning real hyperscaler deployments, and MI400 is on track for 2027. But Nvidia's Blackwell ramp and CUDA ecosystem remain formidable. We analyze whether AMD can capture 20%+ of the AI GPU market.

title: SpaceX: Inside the Hidden Debt and Hype Behind the Biggest IPO in Human History
subtitle: The S-1 is out. SpaceX is going public under SPCX. The valuation target? $1.75 Trillion. The debt load? $29 Billion. Here's what the hype isn't telling you.
summary: SpaceX filed its S-1 for the largest IPO in history under ticker SPCX, targeting a $1.75-2 trillion valuation. But the filings reveal $29.1B in debt, $4.9B net loss last year, and a stunning $7.72B quarterly spend on AI infrastructure through the xAI merger. Starlink generates over $3.25B in quarterly revenue, but that cash is being funneled into the AI arms race against OpenAI. The technology is historic, but the financial math is as risky as it gets.

image.caption: SpaceX Starship on the launchpad at Starbase, Texas

title: Google I/O 2026: 5 Takeaways That Reshape the AI Landscape

title: Cerebras Landed 2026's Biggest IPO. Here's Why Nvidia Isn't Losing Sleep.
